race.comment: GRANDE TERRE (1) last appeared when he finished second over 1312m at Geelong on January 14. Now in the care of new trainer Cindy Brander and has a lot of weight but has the services of Steve Vella, who knows him well, and has won first-up in the past. TANNA (4) progressed nicely last time out to finish just a long neck from the winner in third over 900m at Murray Bridge. Finds a suitable race and can find the winner's circle. GIRLS NIGHT (2) is back after a break of six months. Started the last campaign impressively when in the placings over 1200m at Bordertown. Comes here with definite top-three claims. LISTENING IS DOING (3) was $7 out to $21 first up, but got into the placings, so with the benefit of that run under the belt he can improve further. TORNADO'S SISTER (7) finds it hard to win with just one success from 17 starts. Again tasted defeat last start when tenth over 900m at Murray Bridge, beaten 6 lengths, but finds a suitable race.
